bullets.text: FY2024 organic sales growth 2.2% (below target); CEO Laurent Freixe took over late 2024 with focus on volume recovery.
Portfolio reshaping underway: divesting water brands, doubling down on pet care, coffee (Nespresso), and nutrition as core growth pillars.
AI-driven demand forecasting deployed across 60+ markets as part of the 'Nestlé Compass' digital strategy.
Supply chain nearshoring program targeting 70% local sourcing in key markets by 2026 to reduce logistics costs and carbon footprint.
Private-label competition in Europe and North America pressuring volumes in ambient food and bottled water — key challenge for the new CEO.
FY2024 revenue ~$91.5B, flat YoY; volume declines in North America beverages and snacks signaling consumer fatigue after years of price hikes.
$1.2B cost reduction program launched 2025 — supply chain simplification, SKU rationalization, and headcount reductions.
AI-powered end-to-end supply chain visibility live across top 20 markets, with real-time demand sensing integrated into production planning.
Investing in GLP-1-adjacent innovation (Gatorade reformulations, SodaStream, baked Lay's) to capture demand shift driven by weight-loss drug trends.
Frito-Lay North America volumes fell for the 3rd consecutive quarter in late 2024; responding with entry-price multipacks and smaller-format SKUs.
FY2025 net sales ~$84B, organic growth ~3%; Fabric & Home Care and Beauty divisions leading performance amid pricing normalization.
AI-powered inventory optimization and predictive maintenance deployed at 100+ plants, delivering a 20% reduction in unplanned downtime.
'Reinventing the Value Chain' initiative leveraging SAP S/4HANA for real-time supply chain visibility across 80+ countries; full rollout targeted by 2026.
SK-II, Olay, and Gillette Labs facing significant headwinds in China; P&G accelerating localization of product innovation and marketing.
100% recyclable or reusable packaging commitment by 2030; Tide Eco-Box and refill station pilots live in Europe and North America.
FY2024 net revenue ~$73B; US beef and Pilgrim's Pride (poultry) performing strongly while pork margins remain under commodity cycle pressure.
Completed dual-listing on NYSE in 2024 — a landmark governance milestone improving capital access and international investor profile.
Investing $500M+ annually in automation: AI carcass grading, predictive maintenance, and IoT-enabled cold chain management across global facilities.
ESG scrutiny over Brazil deforestation supply chain links; 'JBS Green' initiative targets zero-deforestation sourcing by 2025 with traceability tech.
Diversifying into alternative proteins (Planterra Foods) and value-added prepared foods to reduce reliance on volatile commodity protein revenues.
FY2024 turnover ~€60B, underlying sales growth 4.2%; volume recovery strongest in India, Indonesia, and Sub-Saharan Africa.
Ice cream division (Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Wall's) being separated as a standalone company by end 2025 — Unilever's largest divestiture in years.
AI-powered demand planning platform across 190 countries reduced forecast error by 15%, cutting working capital and waste.
Refocusing on 30 'Power Brands' (Dove, Hellmann's, Lipton, Comfort) accounting for 70%+ of revenue; underperforming brands being divested.
€800M net productivity savings target by 2026 via SAP S/4HANA rollout and a 30% reduction in the global supplier base.
FY2024 revenue ~$59.4B; volumes -1% globally but EBITDA grew +8% driven by premiumization of Corona, Stella Artois, and Budweiser.
BEES digital B2B platform now connecting 3.5M+ retail customers across 25 markets; processed $12B+ in GMV in 2024.
Middle Africa and South America delivering double-digit revenue growth in 2024, offsetting volume softness in the US and Europe.
$500M+ invested in US sports sponsorships and marketing to rebuild Bud Light brand equity following the 2023 controversy.
Targeting 100% renewable electricity across all breweries by 2025; committed to water-neutral operations in all high water-stress areas by 2030.
FY2024 revenue ~$53B; returned to profitability after FY2023 losses; chicken and prepared foods divisions leading the recovery.
9 plant closures and ~10% workforce reduction executed in 2024 as part of a $1B+ cost-saving restructuring program.
AI-based robotic deboning and portioning systems deployed at 12 major chicken processing plants; targeting 15% reduction in labor costs.
Prepared foods segment (Jimmy Dean, Hillshire Farm, Ball Park) is now the fastest-growing and most profitable division.
Active regulatory and litigation exposure: worker safety, environmental compliance, and ongoing price-fixing investigations in beef and chicken.
FY2024 sales ~€43.5B, like-for-like growth ~5.1%; Dermatological Beauty (CeraVe) and Luxury divisions leading category growth.
'Beauty Tech' strategy: AI tools (ModiFace AR try-on, SkinConsult AI) scanning over 1 billion consumer touchpoints annually.
North Asia (China) growth decelerated in 2024; L'Oréal actively reallocating investment to South/Southeast Asia and the Middle East.
SAP-based integrated planning deployed across 40+ manufacturing sites; pushing toward full Industry 4.0 factory automation.
Active M&A pipeline following the Aesop acquisition (2023, $2.53B); actively targeting prestige skincare assets in the US and Asia.
FY2024 net revenues ~$47.1B, organic growth +12%; long-term organic growth target raised to 5–6% annually through 2028.
Diversification beyond sparkling beverages: sports (Powerade, BodyArmor), RTD coffee (Costa), and alcoholic RTDs (Topo Chico) now account for 25%+ of total volume.
AI-driven vending and cooler optimization ('Coke On' platform) deployed in 30+ markets enabling dynamic pricing and automated restocking.
Consolidated franchise bottler relationships in Africa and Southeast Asia to improve speed-to-market and capital efficiency in high-growth emerging markets.
Targeting 50% recycled packaging material by 2030; EU single-use plastics regulation timelines are accelerating the transition roadmap.
FY2024 revenue ~€36.4B; Heineken and Amstel premium segments grew at double the portfolio average rate.
'EverGreen' strategy delivered €400M in cost savings through supply chain simplification and global procurement consolidation.
AI revenue management tools deployed in 50 markets; 500,000+ connected smart cooler IoT program tracking real-time outlet sell-out data.
Nigeria headwinds persist: local currency devaluation driving double-digit volume declines; Heineken responding with affordability pack formats.
Committed to net-zero by 2040 across the full value chain; 62% of own operations already running on renewable electricity.
